block_01: This is the second part of the IQ Trending annual report. Last week we were taking stock of the year 2020.  With the beginning of the year 2021, investors are struggling to predict where the markets are heading next. So today let’s make some prospects for the future and highlight promising stocks with the potential to grow and anticipated events of the year 2021.
block_03: The year 2020 was one of the best years in recent memory for tech stocks. Through Dec. 31, the information technology sector of the S&P 500 was up about 40% in the calendar year. Investors can't expect technology names to always trounce the market like that, but the rapid digitization of shopping, working and entertainment that the pandemic kicked into high gear will continue to reap rewards for many tech names in 2021.
block_05: The defensive nature of health care stocks is particularly attractive to investors during this unprecedented time. Many are betting on health care stocks that could be winners because of coronavirus-led trends. The long term growth aspect of the healthcare industry is supported by the combination of an aging population and advances in medical sciences and technology. This is why it’s particularly important to have some exposure to health sector stocks in 2021.
block_07: It has been one crazy year for energy stocks as the global coronavirus pandemic has disrupted supply chains, sapped demand, and resulted in extreme volatility for oil and gas prices. But as markets have stabilized into 2020's late innings, oil has bounced back and now sits at roughly $45 a barrel, providing a sign that things are not quite as dire as they were several months ago.
block_09: It was a turbulent year for airline stocks. The COVID-19 pandemic caused travel demand to plummet and left airlines scrambling to raise cash to survive. The year 2021 is starting with a glimmer of hope. No U.S. publicly traded airline fell into bankruptcy as a result of the pandemic, and with vaccines now beginning to roll out, there is a reason for investors and to climb on board.
block_13: The inauguration of Joe Biden as the 46th president of the United States will mark the commencement of the four-year term of Joe Biden as president and Kamala Harris as vice president. A public ceremony is scheduled for Wednesday, January 20, 2021. Once Mr. Biden takes the oath, he has officially assumed office and the power of the presidency will pass into the hands of the democrats for the next four years.
